name: Mannitol
executiveSummary: Osmotic diuresis, toxin elimination, intracranial hypertension.
indications:
## Main indications

Osmotic diuresis, toxin elimination, intracranial hypertension.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: 0.25–1 g/kg IV over 15–30 min; repeat per osmolarity.

pediatrico.notes: Mannitol potentiates the ototoxic effect of aminoglycosides, may reduce the effect of anticoagulant drugs, increases risk of digoxin toxicity (by causing hypokalemia). Reduces renal toxicity of cisplatin. Do not add electrolytes to mannitol solution as precipitation may occur.

stability:
## Stability

- Crystallizes when cold; warm and shake before use.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Headache, confusion, circulatory overload, blurred vision, rhinitis, vomiting, nausea, thirst, urinary retention. Extravasation may cause edema, inflammation, and skin necrosis.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — Mannitol (code 0387, ATC B05BC).
